page: Amazon Web Services m7a.48xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 192 dedicated physical cores and 192 vCPUs utilizing the x86_64 AMD EPYC 9R14 processor running at 3.7 GHz. The memory subsystem consists of 768.0 GB of DDR5 RAM operating at 5 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of memory per core. This instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying instead on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier multi-core CPU performance in stress-ng and Geekbench tests, alongside top-tier memory bandwidth for both cached small blocks and uncached large blocks. The processor supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and cryptography acceleration, contributing to top-tier scores in Passmark encryption and physics tests. However, single-threaded operations such as gzip compression and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression show average performance.

While prompt processing for large language models ranks in the top tier, text generation speeds for smaller models fall into the bottom 25%, highlighting a performance tradeoff in sequential token generation. The lack of local NVMe storage requires external storage configurations, which may impact I/O-heavy workloads. This instance is suited for large-scale general-purpose workloads, software compilation, database operations, and static web serving.
description: Amazon Web Services m7a.48xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 192 dedicated physical cores and 192 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9R14 processor running at 3.7 GHz. It is equipped with 768.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, yielding 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance lacks local storage and GPU hardware, utilizing a 50 Gbps baseline network interface. Benchmark results place its multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and software compilation capabilities in the top tier. Conversely, single-threaded gzip compression and text generation for small language models show average to weak performance. This resource profile is suited for high-density compute tasks, database operations, and static web serving where high memory bandwidth and multi-core scaling are required.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m7a.48xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 192 AMD EPYC cores, 768 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core and memory bandwidth performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m7a.48xlarge features 192 AMD EPYC cores and 768 GB of DDR5 RAM, delivering top-tier multi-core and memory bandwidth performance.
tagline: A high-density, general-purpose instance featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors, DDR5 memory, and top-tier multi-core performance for enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 192 dedicated AMD EPYC 9R14 physical cores running at 3.7 GHz
768.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory
Top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmark performance
50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage or GPU
Suited for software compilation, database operations, and static web serving
